full_text: The next question was, what if you ask for a 360 and you say no? Well, and they say no, I would be very surprised anybody said no. All laposcopic procedure facilities will have the ability to record and take pictures, so it's not unreasonable to expect to be able to see a copy of them. I think so on and all conversations are kept light and you know, understanding on both sides, I don't see it's ever be a problem at all. In fact, every professional I know would be more than keen to discuss it with you so that you understand. I'm a great believer in photos, I often get told off. I'll do more photos than everyone already, but I think it's so important because it helps you visualize as the person who's directly affected by it, where they'd see is and how it's affecting you and why you may be having the symptoms that you have. We show all very individual to each person.
